subject: Re: [Deel x Eragon] Sample Workflows
body_plain:
Analyse zoominfo vendor contract. Start by searching my Gmail (last 90
days) and Slack for any conversations involving this vendor: extract
pricing discussed, commitments made, and internal sentiment. Then extract
all commercial terms from the contract (TCV, payment terms, auto-renewal,
price cap). Check compliance against our 3 non-negotiables: auto-renewal
opt-out, minimum Net 30 payment terms, and renewal price cap ≤3% or CPI.
Flag any items requiring Legal review. Then give me a prioritised
negotiation strategy with 3–5 levers: specific asks, rationale, and
estimated impact, informed by what you found in Gmail and Slack. Close with
a Final Verdict: Approved, Approved with Conditions, or Blocked.

subject: Workspace "Eragon-Deployment-1" Monthly Claude API Spend Exceeding Thresholds: API Usage Paused Until June 1, 2026
body_plain:
Hello,

We wanted to alert you that your account balance has crossed the $255000.00 threshold you set as the total monthly API spend for the Eragon-Deployment-1 workspace in your organization "Eragon." Per your configuration, further API usage is now paused for this workspace until midnight UTC on June 1, 2026.

If you'd like to modify your alert settings at any time, you can visit your Alert Settings page in your account dashboard. There, you can remove this limit to immediately re-enable API access for this workspace. Otherwise, API access will be re-enabled at the start of next month.

Warmly,

The Anthropic Team

For any further questions, please visit our help center.

Claude.ai · Research · Products · Company

subject: Introducing Claude Opus 4.8
body_plain:
Our most intelligent Opus model yet, with improvements in coding and agentic capabilities, and 1M context.

Today, we're introducing Claude Opus 4.8 <https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-8?utm_content=link>, our most intelligent Opus model and the best generally available model for coding, agents, and demanding knowledge work.

Here's what's new on the Claude Platform (API):

* Claude Opus 4.8: The latest version of Opus, with improvements in coding, long-running autonomous agents, and reasoning on complex enterprise problems. Available at $5 input / $25 output per million tokens.
* Fast mode (research preview): Fast mode for Opus 4.8 delivers full Opus 4.8 intelligence with roughly 2.5x faster output token generation. Fast mode now costs less than before, at 2x standard Opus 4.8 pricing. Reach out to your account team to get started or request access here <https://claude.com/fast-mode?utm_content=inline_link>.
* Mid-conversation system messages <https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/mid-conversation-system-messages?utm_content=inline_link>: The Messages API now accepts system entries inside the messages array, so you can update Claude's instructions mid-task without breaking the prompt cache.
* Self-hosted sandboxes for Claude Managed Agents (public beta) <https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/managed-agents/self-hosted-sandboxes?utm_content=inline_link>: Build and deploy agents at scale while keeping sensitive files, packages, and services in your own infrastructure or with a managed sandbox provider.
* MCP tunnels (research preview) <https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agents-and-tools/mcp-tunnels/overview?utm_content=inline_link>: Connect agents to MCP servers inside your private network without exposing them to the public internet. Request access here <https://claude.com/form/claude-managed-agents?utm_content=inline_link>.

For Claude Code users:

* Claude Opus 4.8 is now the default fallback model <https://code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config?utm_content=inline_link> in Claude Code. It defaults to high effort, which is the best balance of quality and speed, costing roughly the same as Opus 4.7 on xhigh effort.
* Dynamic workflows <https://claude.com/blog/introducing-dynamic-workflows-in-claude-code?utm_content=inline_link> are now available in Claude Code, which help Claude tackle extraordinarily large tasks, executing across 10s to 100s of parallel subagents. They are on by default, and can be toggled off by adding "disableWorkflows: true" to your managed settings. See the blog for more details.

Read the blog <https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-8?utm_content=hero_cta#ctaButton>

Opus 4.8 is available on our API, Amazon Web Services, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Foundry.

To learn more, check out our developer docs <https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/models/overview?utm_content=inline_link>, prompt engineering best practices <https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/prompt-engineering/claude-prompting-best-practices?utm_content=inline_link>, and pricing <https://claude.com/pricing?utm_content=inline_link#api>.

Anthropic PBC, 548 Market St, PMB 903375, San Francisco, CA 94104

This email was sent to lance@eragon.ai. To opt out of future emails, click unsubscribe <https://links.email.claude.com/s/u/pJ90Bw7IhXmCUUlAOmt0D2j9XbiQPRO6ZWjj7vkiEvPKe9EEMcMuCM0Ee1zUl2Dfh_w9uWuXrg8lxPy06Dnxf20J1akk3GXLiRp9wxFF6Hyg3CkMdh_XfOM1xLUHbqpNTmT1XTlNFzTQCmonS-3QH150nYwIONzz_GPvhT93kJguJLy73Vi9J2o1SzxFx1KwFp4jomf7xq5kiTUP/kNZKIT83Veg6s0ZtSlzK6RF3t40ikATq/21#footerUnsub>.
snippet: Introducing Claude Opus 4.8 — our most intelligent Opus model yet, with improvements in coding and agentic capabilities, and 1M context. Available at $5 input / $25 output per million tokens.

subject: 30 seconds to your first response
body_plain:
Hi Lance,

The fastest path from signup to a working response is two code blocks. Copy one, swap your key, run it. If you already use the OpenAI SDK, the only line that changes is base_url.

Open the quickstart ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/quickstart?utm_source=email&utm_medium=lifecycle&utm_campaign=onboarding_v2&utm_content=b1_first_request&utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=B1-1+First+Response&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

PYTHON

from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI(
  base_url="https://openrouter.ai/api/v1",
  api_key="YOUR_API_KEY"
)

response = client.chat.completions.create(
  model="openai/gpt-4o-mini",
  messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}]
)

print(response.choices[0].message.content)

CURL

curl https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model":"openai/gpt-4o-mini","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Hello"}]}'

What the response looks like. A standard OpenAI chat completion object. choices[0].message.content holds the text. usage has token counts. model shows which model actually served the request, which matters when you use openrouter/auto and want to see what the router picked. If you already read OpenAI responses, you already know how to read these.

If the first call fails. A 401 is almost always the key header. A 402 means the free-tier daily cap was hit or credits are needed for a paid model. A 404 on a model string usually means dots vs hyphens; OpenRouter slugs use dots, so claude-sonnet-4.6 not claude-sonnet-4-6. The models ( https://openrouter.ai/models ) page has every valid slug.

Free tier: 10 requests per day across 29 free models. No credit card to start.

openrouter.ai ( https://openrouter.ai ) · Discord ( https://discord.com/invite/8TpuzNXQYr ) · X ( https://x.com/openrouter ) · Docs ( https://openrouter.ai/docs )
snippet: The fastest path from signup to a working response is two code blocks. Copy one, swap your key, run it. If you already use the OpenAI SDK, the only line that changes is base_url.

subject: Your agent can't act without this
body_plain:
Hey Lance Mathias,

Ever built an AI agent that can read everything but can't actually do anything?

That's the tool call problem. Your agent can understand "update the deal in Salesforce" but has no hands to make it happen.

Agent Handler gives your AI the ability to take actions on thousands of tools across dozens of connectors. Here's how to make your first one:

Enable your first tool call (5 min) ( https://ah.merge.dev/get-started )

The average developer makes their first authenticated call in under 10 minutes. Here's the steps:

1. Pick a connector
2. Add your credentials
3. Watch your agent take action

- Team Merge

Merge Agent Handler

subject: What comes with a paid key
body_plain:
Hi Lance,

A paid key gives you the full OpenRouter catalog. 300+ models from 60+ providers, including every frontier model: Claude, GPT, Gemini, Llama, and the rest. Same API, same code, same key.

Add credits ( https://openrouter.ai/credits )

WHAT SHIPS WITH A PAID KEY

Provider fallback. When a provider goes down, your request re-routes to another provider serving the same model. No retry logic on your end, no downtime for your users. Prompt caching. Repeated context gets cached on supported models. Cuts repeated-context cost by up to 90%.

AND MORE

Model variants. Append :nitro for speed, :floor for the cheapest provider, or :exacto for precision routing. Same model, different optimization, one string change. Dedicated rate limits. Free-tier keys share a pool. Paid keys get their own limits, so other users' traffic never touches yours. No daily request cap. Free tier limits you to 10 requests per day. Paid keys have no ceiling.

How pricing works. No provider markup. You pay the per-token rate each provider sets. The activity dashboard ( https://openrouter.ai/activity ) shows every call with its model, tokens, and cost in real time.

Pricing for every model is on the catalog at openrouter.ai/models ( https://openrouter.ai/models ). No minimum purchase. Credits expire after one year. If you want the lowest possible spend without micro-managing models, openrouter/auto selects the best model for each request from a pool of 26+ options.

openrouter.ai ( https://openrouter.ai ) · Discord ( https://discord.com/invite/8TpuzNXQYr ) · X ( https://x.com/openrouter ) · Docs ( https://openrouter.ai/docs )
snippet: A paid key gives you the full OpenRouter catalog. 300+ models from 60+ providers, including every frontier model: Claude, GPT, Gemini, Llama, and the rest.
